Status
Outreach
§ 01Who We Are

Forhemit is a California Public Benefit Corporation that guides business owners through ESOP transitions. We exist to help owners who want to sell their companies to their employees — preserving legacy, protecting jobs, and creating broad-based ownership.

Unlike private equity acquirers or traditional M&A brokers, Forhemit is structured as a stewardship firm. Our incentives are aligned with the long-term success of the company and its employees, not short-term extraction.

§ 02The §1042 Opportunity

Internal Revenue Code §1042 allows a business owner who sells to an ESOP to defer 100% of the federal capital gains tax on the sale — provided the proceeds are reinvested in Qualified Replacement Securities (QRS) within 540 days. This is one of the most powerful tax deferral strategies available to business owners, yet most CPAs are not aware of it.

Key fact: The §1042 election is permanent once made. The deferred gain is never taxed during the seller's lifetime — it is forgiven at death under current law. This is not a deferral that later reverses; it is a permanent step-up in basis for the seller's heirs.

For CPAs, this creates a significant advisory opportunity: the ability to help clients achieve a tax-efficient exit that also fulfills their values. The §1042 election requires that the CPA verify basis, holding period, and reinvestment timing — all areas where a CPA's involvement is essential.

§ 03Client Retention

When a business owner sells to a private equity firm, the CPA typically loses the client. The PE firm brings in its own accounting team, and the relationship ends.

An ESOP transaction is different. The company continues to operate independently. The existing CPA relationship is valued and often retained. By advising your client on ESOP as an exit option, you preserve a relationship that would otherwise be lost.

Bottom line: Recommending an ESOP is not just good advice for your client — it is good business for your firm. You keep the client, deepen the advisory relationship, and position yourself as the go-to advisor for the company's post-close accounting needs.
§ 04What We Need

We are looking for CPAs who work with business owners who are considering an exit in the next one to three years. Specifically:

A.Owners aged 55+ who have not yet identified a successor or succession plan
B.Companies with stable or growing EBITDA, ideally above $1 million annually
C.Owners who care about their employees and legacy, not just price
D.C-Corporations or companies willing to convert to C-Corp status for the §1042 election
§ 05Referral Model

Our referral process is simple and obligation-free:

1.You identify a client who may be a fit for an ESOP transition
2.You introduce them to Forhemit — a warm email or a call is all it takes
3.Forhemit conducts a confidential qualification assessment at no cost to the owner
4.If the deal closes, you receive a referral fee as a percentage of the transaction

There is no cost to you or your client for the initial assessment. If the deal does not proceed, the introduction was still valuable — it demonstrated that you are thinking proactively about your client's exit planning.

§ 06Next Steps

If you work with business owners who may benefit from learning about ESOP transitions, we would welcome a brief conversation. A 15-minute call is all we need to explain the opportunity and answer your questions.
